collections.name: Agamas
collections.description: Canonical scriptures of Jainism comprising the teachings of the Tirthankaras, including sutras on ethics, cosmology, and monastic discipline preserved in Ardhamagadhi Prakrit.

collections.name: Avesta - Yasna and Gathas
collections.description: Ancient Zoroastrian liturgical texts and hymns, including the Yasna ritual prayers and the Gathas—philosophical poems attributed to Zoroaster himself—foundational to Zoroastrian doctrine and worship.

collections.name: Bible and Translations
collections.description: The complete Christian scriptures in multiple language translations, spanning the Old Testament, New Testament, and various theological versions from ancient Greek and Hebrew sources to modern English renderings.

collections.name: Core Tablets
collections.description: Foundational Bahá'í scriptures written by Bahá'u'lláh and 'Abdu'l-Bahá, including divine revelations, spiritual teachings, and guidance on individual and community life.

collections.name: Five Classics
collections.description: Ancient Chinese philosophical texts foundational to Confucian thought, comprising the I Ching, Shijing, Shujing, Liji, and Chunqiu with their commentaries and interpretations.

collections.name: Foundational Classics
collections.description: Core Taoist philosophical texts including the Daodejing, Zhuangzi, and related early writings that establish fundamental concepts of the Dao, natural spontaneity, and non-action.

collections.name: Foundational Texts
collections.description: Core Islamic scriptures including the Qur'an, hadith collections, and early theological works that establish Islamic doctrine and law.

collections.name: Four Books
collections.description: Core philosophical texts of Confucianism comprising the Analects, Mencius, Great Learning, and Doctrine of the Mean—essential writings on ethics, governance, and self-cultivation.

collections.name: Guru Granth Sahib
collections.description: The Sikh scripture composed of hymns, poems, and teachings from the ten Sikh Gurus and saints, written in various Indian languages and preserved in Gurmukhi script. This sacred text serves as the eternal Guru of Sikhism and is central to all religious and community practices.

collections.name: Mahayana Sutras
collections.description: Mahayana Buddhist scriptures emphasizing the bodhisattva path and universal salvation, including the Lotus Sutra, Pure Land texts, and teachings on Buddha-nature.

collections.name: Pali Canon
collections.description: The Pali Canon comprises the oldest surviving Buddhist scriptures in the Theravada tradition, organized into three divisions: the Suttas (discourses), Vinaya (monastic rules), and Abhidhamma (philosophical analysis).

collections.name: Torah and Tanakh
collections.description: Hebrew Bible texts comprising the Torah (Five Books of Moses), Prophets (Nevi'im), and Writings (Ketuvim), the foundational scriptures of Judaism.

collections.name: Vedas and Upanishads
collections.description: Ancient Sanskrit scriptures foundational to Hinduism, comprising the four Vedas (Rigveda, Yajurveda, Samaveda, Atharvaveda) and the philosophical Upanishads that explore ultimate reality and the nature of Brahman.

collections.name: Ali ibn Abi Talib
collections.description: Sermons, letters, and teachings attributed to Ali ibn Abi Talib, the fourth Caliph and cousin of Prophet Muhammad, central to Shia Islamic theology and jurisprudence.

collections.name: Avesta - Khordeh Avesta
collections.description: Essential Zoroastrian prayers and liturgical texts in Avestan language, including the Gathas, Yasna, and daily devotional formulas for worship and ritual practice.

collections.name: Avesta - Yashts
collections.description: Hymnic prayers and invocations to Zoroastrian divine beings (yazatas), from the liturgical Avestan texts. These deity-focused compositions form part of the Zoroastrian scriptural tradition.

collections.name: Core Talks
collections.description: Recorded speeches and addresses by Baha'i leaders and teachers on faith principles, community life, and spiritual development. These talks explore core Baha'i teachings including unity, progressive revelation, and the oneness of humanity.

collections.name: Epics
collections.description: Ancient Sanskrit narratives including the Mahabharata and Ramayana, which are foundational Hindu texts depicting dharma, heroic quests, and divine incarnations through epic storytelling.

collections.name: Sacred Poetry
collections.description: Devotional hymns and poetic compositions from Sikh gurus and saints, primarily from the Guru Granth Sahib and other sacred texts, emphasizing divine love and spiritual guidance.

collections.name: Shi'ah Imams
collections.description: Writings and teachings of the Twelve Imams, including their sermons, letters, and sayings, central to Shi'ah Islamic theology and spiritual guidance.

collections.name: Tablet Translations
collections.description: Translations of the Báb's and Bahá'u'lláh's tablets—foundational scriptural writings of the Bahá'í faith addressing theology, law, and spiritual guidance.

collections.name: Talmud and Mishnah
collections.description: Rabbinic teachings and legal discussions from the Mishnah and Talmud, encompassing Jewish law, ethics, and biblical interpretation. These foundational texts record centuries of scholarly debate on halakha (Jewish practice) and aggadah (narrative and philosophy).

collections.name: Traditions
collections.description: Islamic jurisprudential and theological works representing the major madhabs (schools of law) including Hanafi, Maliki, Shafi'i, and Hanbali traditions, along with Shi'a and other Islamic legal methodologies.

collections.name: Apocrypha and Early Texts
collections.description: Early Christian writings outside the biblical canon, including gospels, acts, epistles, and apocalypses from the first centuries CE that offer diverse theological perspectives and historical context for Christian origins.

collections.name: Avesta - Visperad and Vendidad
collections.description: Zoroastrian liturgical and legal texts: the Visperad contains ritual invocations and offerings to divine beings, while the Vendidad prescribes laws, rituals, and purification practices against spiritual contamination.

collections.name: Avesta - Visperad and Yashts
collections.description: Zoroastrian liturgical and devotional texts including the Visperad (ritual prayers for ceremonies and seasonal festivals) and Yashts (hymns of praise to divine beings and natural forces).

collections.name: Classical Commentary
collections.description: Medieval and early modern exegetical works interpreting Confucian classics, including commentaries on the Four Books and Five Classics by scholars from the Song, Ming, and Qing dynasties.

collections.name: Compilations
collections.description: Official collections of Bahá'í teachings and writings compiled by the religion's central institutions, organizing core doctrines, guidance, and spiritual principles for study and application.

collections.name: Core Publications
collections.description: Foundational writings of Baha'u'llah, the Bab, and Abdu'l-Baha that form the spiritual and theological basis of the Baha'i Faith, including revealed texts, letters, and tablets.

collections.name: Daozang Scriptures Shangqing and Lingbao
collections.description: Daoist scripture compilations from the Shangqing (Supreme Purity) and Lingbao (Numinous Treasure) traditions, featuring revealed texts, talismans, rituals, and cosmological teachings central to medieval Chinese religious practice.

collections.name: Halakhah and Legal Codes
collections.description: Jewish legal texts interpreting and codifying Torah law, including Talmudic discussions, responsa, and medieval codes like Maimonides' Mishneh Torah and the Shulchan Aruch.

collections.name: Midrash
collections.description: Rabbinic interpretations and homiletical expositions on Torah and other Hebrew Bible texts, exploring legal, ethical, and mystical dimensions through creative exegesis and storytelling traditions.

collections.name: Puranas
collections.description: Ancient Hindu mythological and devotional texts narrating stories of deities, cosmology, and dharma across multiple literary traditions. Includes the Mahabharata, Ramayana, and eighteen major Puranas.

collections.name: Zen and Chan
collections.description: Zen and Chan Buddhist texts emphasizing direct insight, meditation practice, and teacher-student dialogues including koans, records of masters, and commentaries on enlightenment.

collections.name: Biblical Commentary
collections.description: Rabbinic interpretations and exegetical works on the Hebrew Bible, spanning from medieval Jewish scholars to modern commentators who elucidate Torah, Prophets, and Writings.

collections.name: Church Fathers
collections.description: Writings from early Christian theologians and bishops (2nd-8th centuries) including doctrinal treatises, apologetic works, and scriptural commentaries that shaped orthodox Christian theology.

collections.name: Classical Commentaries and Philosophy
collections.description: Classical Chinese commentaries on Daoist texts including the Daodejing and Zhuangzi, featuring philosophical interpretations from major Daoist schools and traditions.

collections.name: Doctrine and Theology
collections.description: Islamic theological treatises and doctrinal works examining core beliefs including tawhid (divine unity), prophethood, and eschatology across Sunni, Shia, and other schools of Islamic thought.

collections.name: Eastern Orthodox
collections.description: Eastern Orthodox theological and liturgical texts including patristic writings, liturgical services, icons theology, and ecclesiastical documents from the Eastern Christian tradition.

collections.name: Huang-Lao and Syncretic Texts
collections.description: Daoist texts blending Huang-Lao philosophy with Confucian and Legalist thought, including early imperial commentaries and syncretistic works from the Han dynasty.

collections.name: Jurisprudence
collections.description: Islamic legal treatises and rulings across the major schools of Islamic law (Hanafi, Maliki, Shafi'i, Hanbali), covering principles of jurisprudence, fatwa collections, and applications of Sharia to religious and civil matters.

collections.name: Kabbalah and Mysticism
collections.description: Jewish mystical texts exploring divine mysteries, cosmic structures, and spiritual practices, including Zohar commentaries, Lurianic writings, and meditative teachings on God's hidden dimensions.

collections.name: Pahlavi Literature
collections.description: Middle Persian religious and philosophical texts from the Sassanid era, including Zoroastrian theological commentaries, ritual manuals, and cosmological teachings preserved in Pahlavi script.

collections.name: Philosophy
collections.description: Foundational texts of Hindu philosophical schools including Upanishads, Brahma Sutras, and Bhagavad Gita, exploring metaphysics, epistemology, and the nature of Brahman and Atman.

collections.name: Philosophy
collections.description: Foundational Jain philosophical texts exploring metaphysics, epistemology, and the nature of karma, soul, and liberation within the Jain tradition.

collections.name: Pilgrim Notes
collections.description: Personal accounts and letters from Bahá'í pilgrims describing their visits to Bahá'í holy sites and meetings with Bahá'í leaders during the late 19th and early 20th centuries.

collections.name: Second Temple Literature
collections.description: Jewish texts composed during the Second Temple period (515 BCE–70 CE), including apocryphal works, pseudepigrapha, and sectarian documents like the Dead Sea Scrolls.

collections.name: Alchemy and Self-Cultivation
collections.description: Taoist texts on internal alchemy (neidan), qi cultivation, and spiritual transformation through meditation and breathing practices. These works blend cosmology, physiology, and mystical philosophy to guide practitioners toward immortality and union with the Tao.

collections.name: Bhakti and Devotional
collections.description: Hindu devotional texts emphasizing personal devotion to deities through prayer, poetry, and theological interpretation. Includes bhakti movement literature, hymns, and philosophical works on devotional practice from various Hindu traditions.

collections.name: Ethics and Practice
collections.description: Jain texts on ethical conduct and daily religious practice, including vows, dietary rules, meditation techniques, and guidance for lay practitioners and monks seeking spiritual purification.

collections.name: Han-Tang Confucianism
collections.description: Confucian philosophical and commentarial texts from the Han through Tang dynasties, including interpretations of the Five Classics and works on ritual, governance, and moral cultivation by scholars like Zheng Xuan and Kong Yingda.

collections.name: Hasidism and Musar
collections.description: Hasidic and Musar spiritual teachings emphasizing personal piety, ethical self-improvement, and devotional practices within Jewish tradition. Includes rebbes' discourses, moral instruction texts, and meditative works on inner spiritual transformation.

collections.name: Internal Alchemy (Neidan)
collections.description: Taoist texts on inner cultivation practices that transform vital energy (qi), essence (jing), and spirit (shen) through meditation, breathing, and visualization techniques.

collections.name: Jewish Philosophy and Theology
collections.description: Medieval and modern Jewish philosophical texts exploring God, ethics, and Jewish law through rationalist and mystical approaches, including works by Maimonides, Spinoza, and contemporary theologians.

collections.name: Medieval Theology
collections.description: Latin theological treatises, commentaries, and disputations from Christian scholastic philosophers (11th-15th centuries), including works on doctrine, natural philosophy, and biblical interpretation.

collections.name: Mysticism
collections.description: Islamic mystical texts exploring the inner spiritual dimensions of faith, including Sufi poetry, philosophical treatises on divine love, and teachings on spiritual disciplines and states of union with the Divine.

collections.name: Mysticism and Devotion
collections.description: Medieval and early modern Christian writings exploring contemplative prayer, union with God, and the spiritual experiences of mystics. Includes works on apophatic theology, devotional practices, and firsthand accounts from Christian contemplatives.

collections.name: Neo-Confucianism
collections.description: Medieval and early modern Confucian philosophical texts emphasizing metaphysical principles (li), the investigation of things, and moral cultivation through cosmological harmony and self-refinement.

collections.name: Philosophy
collections.description: Islamic philosophical works exploring metaphysics, epistemology, and ethics through the synthesis of Greek philosophy with Quranic theology, including treatises by medieval and contemporary Muslim philosophers.

collections.name: Practice and Meditation
collections.description: Buddhist texts on meditation techniques, mindfulness practices, and path to enlightenment across Theravada, Mahayana, and Tantric traditions. Includes sutras, commentaries, and practical guides for cultivating mental discipline.

collections.name: Reformation
collections.description: Sixteenth-century Protestant theological works, polemics, and biblical commentaries by reformers including Luther, Calvin, and Zwingli challenging Catholic doctrine and ecclesiastical authority.

collections.name: Related Mystical Traditions
collections.description: Sufi Islamic texts, Hindu Vedantic writings, and other mystical philosophies that influenced and paralleled Sikh devotional theology. These works explore the spiritual concepts of divine union and transcendence central to Sikhism's synthesis of religious traditions.

collections.name: Sikh Philosophy
collections.description: Theological and philosophical works exploring Sikh doctrine, ethics, and spiritual concepts from the Guru Granth Sahib and related scholarly interpretations of Sikhism's monotheistic foundations and social principles.

collections.name: Baha'i Books
collections.description: Foundational writings of the Baha'i faith, including the works of Baha'u'llah and the Báb, along with interpretive texts and teachings on unity, progressive revelation, and spiritual development.

collections.name: Ethics and Governance
collections.description: Confucian texts on moral philosophy, virtuous conduct, and principles for effective governance, including foundational works on ritual propriety, filial piety, and statecraft.

collections.name: External Alchemy Medicine and Longevity
collections.description: Taoist texts on external alchemical practices, herbal medicine, and longevity techniques, including formulas for elixirs, mineral preparations, and physiological methods aimed at achieving immortality.

collections.name: Hagiography History and Ethics
collections.description: Biographical accounts and ethical teachings of Taoist immortals, sages, and deified figures, alongside histories of Taoist traditions and moral instruction texts guiding spiritual practice.

collections.name: History and Biography
collections.description: Historical accounts and biographical narratives of the Buddha, Buddhist teachers, and monastic communities from various traditions, including hagiographies and chronicles of Buddhism's development across Asia.

collections.name: History and Biography
collections.description: Islamic historical narratives and biographical accounts of the Prophet Muhammad, his companions, and early Muslim leaders, including hadith collections, sira literature, and chronicles of Islamic empires.

collections.name: History and Scholarship
collections.description: Academic studies and historical analyses of Zoroastrianism, including scholarly works on the religion's origins, development, and influence on other faiths. Covers Zoroastrian communities, texts, and religious practices across different periods and regions.

collections.name: Indian Tales and Literature
collections.description: Sanskrit and vernacular narratives including the Mahabharata, Ramayana, Puranas, and folk tales that transmit Hindu philosophy, ethics, and divine stories through epic poetry and mythology.

collections.name: Liturgy and Poetry
collections.description: Jewish prayers, blessings, and liturgical texts used in worship services, along with religious and devotional poetry spanning medieval to modern periods.

collections.name: Narrative and Biography
collections.description: Jain texts narrating the lives of the Tirthankaras and other enlightened figures, including their spiritual journeys and teachings. These biographical accounts serve as models for practicing Jain ethics and asceticism.

collections.name: Quanzhen and Later Taoist Schools
collections.description: Philosophical and liturgical texts from Quanzhen and subsequent Taoist schools, including commentaries on the Daodejing, ritual manuals, and internal alchemy treatises from the 12th century onward.

collections.name: Sanskrit Drama and Poetry
collections.description: Classical Sanskrit dramatic works and poetry spanning epics, plays, and lyrical compositions from Hindu literary tradition, including texts like the Mahabharata and Ramayana alongside court dramas and devotional verse.

collections.name: Sermons and Preaching
collections.description: Christian sermons and homilies spanning various denominations, periods, and theological traditions. Includes expository preaching, doctrinal instruction, and pastoral addresses from church fathers to contemporary ministers.

collections.name: Sikh History
collections.description: Historical accounts of the ten Gurus, the development of Sikhism, and key events in Sikh communities from the 15th century onward. Includes biographical narratives, chronicles of battles, and records of institutional growth.

collections.name: Study Guides
collections.description: Interpretive guides and educational materials examining Bahá'í scripture, theology, and practice. These resources help readers understand core teachings on unity, progressive revelation, and the Bahá'í vision of social transformation.

collections.name: Swedenborg and Visionary Theology
collections.description: Theological writings and spiritual visions by Emanuel Swedenborg exploring Christian mysticism, divine revelation, and esoteric interpretations of scripture through accounts of heavenly encounters and inner spiritual experience.

collections.name: Tagore
collections.description: Works by Rabindranath Tagore, the Bengali Hindu philosopher and poet, including devotional poetry, essays on spirituality and social reform, and philosophical writings blending Hindu thought with modern themes.

collections.name: Taoist Ethics and Morality
collections.description: Taoist texts exploring ethical principles, moral cultivation, and virtuous living within the context of natural harmony and the Tao. Includes philosophical treatises, precept lists, and commentaries on righteous action and spiritual development.

collections.name: The Sikh Religion (Macauliffe)
collections.description: Max Arthur Macauliffe's comprehensive English translations of Sikh scripture and history, including the Guru Granth Sahib and biographical accounts of the ten Gurus, with scholarly commentary on Sikh doctrine and practice.

collections.name: Administrative
collections.description: Baha'i administrative texts including constitutional documents, institutional guidelines, and leadership directives that establish governance structures and operational procedures for Baha'i communities worldwide.

collections.name: Baha'i Talks
collections.description: Recorded and transcribed addresses by Baha'i teachers and scholars discussing Baha'i principles, theology, and spiritual practice. These talks cover topics ranging from foundational beliefs to contemporary applications of Baha'i teachings.

collections.name: Modern
collections.description: Contemporary Hindu philosophical writings, spiritual commentaries, and interpretive essays from 20th-century thinkers engaging with traditional Vedantic and devotional teachings in modern contexts.

collections.name: Modern Theology
collections.description: Twentieth and twenty-first century Christian theological works examining doctrine, faith, and practice through contemporary philosophical, social, and scientific perspectives.

collections.name: Reference
collections.description: Scholarly commentaries, concordances, and study guides to Baha'i sacred texts and teachings. Includes historical overviews, bibliographies, and interpretive resources for understanding core Baha'i doctrine and practice.

collections.name: Encyclopedic References
collections.description: Comprehensive reference works on Sikhism including dictionaries, encyclopedias, and scholarly guides to Sikh history, theology, and practice. These texts provide detailed explanations of Sikh concepts, historical figures, and religious institutions.

collections.name: Miscellaneous
collections.description: Islamic texts covering diverse topics including hadith collections, legal treatises, theological works, and devotional literature from various schools of Islamic thought.

collections.name: Papers
collections.description: Scholarly and analytical papers examining Baha'i theology, history, and teachings across diverse topics including social ethics, scripture interpretation, and interfaith dialogue.

collections.name: News
collections.description: Baha'i news articles, announcements, and reports documenting community events, administrative developments, and faith-related current affairs within the Baha'i tradition.
